title: The Passions of the Soul
description: The Passions of the Soul — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "论灵魂的激情" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Table of Contents"
memes.description: "Complete table of contents for The Passions of the Soul by René Descartes (1649)"

memes.label: Anger (Colère)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中论述的一种激情，源于对某种恶的判断，伴随身体中血液与动物精神的剧烈运动。与憎恨(hatred)关系密切但性质不同——愤怒基于直接伤害行为，涉及报复冲动。核心论述见 Articles 25、46、65、199。

memes.label: Animal Spirits (Esprits Animaux)
memes.description: The animal spirits (esprits animaux) are the finest, most agitated particles of the blood, filtered in the brain and serving as the mechanical medium through which the soul moves the body and receives its passions — the central physiological concept in Descartes' mind-body theory.

memes.label: Aristotle (384–322 BCE)
memes.description: Ancient Greek philosopher whose authority dominated the medieval Schools and whose treatment of the passions Descartes explicitly rejected as "skimpy and mostly so implausible" — motivating the entire methodological departure of The Passions of the Soul.

memes.description: "Article 99 of The Passions of the Soul — Part II"

memes.label: Aversion (Aversion / Évitement)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的厌恶/回避——欲望的反面，灵魂对某个对象"远离"的倾向，与欲望构成激情的基本方向对子。

memes.label: Boldness (Hardiesse)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中与恐惧(fear)或惊骇(terror)直接对立的激情。在 Article 59 被定义为恐惧的反面，并在 Articles 171–176 中被详细分析为一种"勇气的过量"。与勇气(courage)、好胜(emulation)密切相关，其极端化形式可能导致鲁莽或不德。

memes.label: Confidence (Confiance / Assurance)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的自信——当恐惧消退或希望增强时的一种激情状态，是恐惧的变体，与果敢和希望密切相关。

memes.label: Contempt (Mépris)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中与敬重(esteem)对立的激情，灵魂对对象卑微或渺小的倾向性表象；由强化"渺小"印象的动物精气运动引起。核心论述见 Articles 54–55、149–150。

memes.label: Courage (Courage)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中与恐惧(fear)直接对立的激情。在 Article 59 中被定义为畏缩的反面；大胆(boldness)则是恐惧或惊骇的反面。笛卡尔进一步区分了勇气(courage)、大胆(boldness)和好胜(emulation)三个相关概念。核心论述见 Articles 39-40、59、171-176。

memes.label: Curiosity (Curiosité)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的好奇心——一种求知的欲望，源于惊奇，是科学探索的动力来源，但也可能沦为"盲目的好奇"。

memes.label: The Decii (Roman heroes)
memes.description: Three heroes of one ancient Roman family who died in battle the same way decades apart, cited by Descartes in Article 173 as the exemplar of boldness — showing how one act can join despair about its object (certain death) with hope about its goal (inspiring victory).

memes.label: Democritus (c. 460–370 BCE)
memes.description: Ancient Greek atomist philosopher, known in Descartes' Passions as "the laughing philosopher" whose laughter at human folly contrasts with Heraclitus' tears, illustrating individual temperament differences in passion expression.

memes.label: Derision (Moquerie/Dérision)
memes.description: A species of joy mixed with hatred, arising from perceiving a small misfortune in someone we think deserves it. Descartes analyzes derision as a morally ambiguous passion closely connected to laughter, wonder, and indignation.

memes.label: Desire (Le Désir)
memes.description: Desire (le désir) is one of the six primitive passions — a future-directed agitation of the soul caused by the animal spirits that impels us to obtain a good or avoid an evil, distinct from love (which joins to a present object) and from volition (which originates in the soul alone).

memes.label: Despair (Désespoir)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中希望(hope)的极端反面。Article 58 将其定义为"希望之反面——当希望的可能性完全消失时"；Article 166 进一步论述了希望向绝望转化的心理—生理机制。与悲伤(sadness)、焦虑(anxiety)密切相关，是激情由"趋"向"避"转换的关键节点。

memes.label: Envy (Envie)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中的一种激情，混杂恨的悲伤，因看到善好归于被认为不配之人而产生；笛卡尔区分了作为恶习的 envy 与作为激情的 envy。核心论述见 Articles 182–184。

memes.label: Esteem (Estime)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中惊异(wonder)的派生激情，灵魂对对象价值的倾向性表象；与蔑视(contempt)构成对立组。核心论述见 Articles 54–55、149–154。

memes.label: Fear (Crainte)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中的一种原始激情，源于灵魂对可能发生之恶的判断。与希望对立，与 courage 互为反面。当恐惧极端化时变为绝望(despair)，当对象取决于我们时表现为畏缩或恐惧。核心论述见 Articles 36、38-40、48、59。

memes.label: Free Will (Libre Arbitre)
memes.description: In Descartes' philosophy, free will is the soul's absolute and inalienable power over its own volitions. It is the sole ground of human dignity, the foundation of all moral responsibility, and the basis of the virtue of generosity.

memes.label: Generosity (Générosité)
memes.description: A central virtue in Descartes' moral philosophy — the knowledge of one's free will and the firm resolution to use it well.

memes.label: Glory (Gloire)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的荣耀与虚荣——一种基于自爱的喜悦，与自尊、羞耻和对他人的评价密切相关。

memes.label: Gratitude (Reconnaissance)
memes.description: A species of love aroused by someone's action that has done us good, accompanied by a desire to reciprocate. Descartes classifies gratitude as both a passion and a virtue—"one of the principal bonds of human society"—and contrasts it with ingratitude, which he denies is a passion at all.

memes.label: Hatred (La Haine)
memes.description: Hatred (la haine) is one of the six primitive passions in Descartes' taxonomy — a commotion of the soul caused by the animal spirits that inclines the soul to separate itself from objects judged harmful, directly opposed to love.

memes.label: Heraclitus (c. 535–475 BCE)
memes.description: Pre-Socratic philosopher of Ephesus, known in Descartes' Passions as "the weeping philosopher" whose tears at human folly contrast with Democritus' laughter, illustrating individual temperament differences in passion expression.

memes.label: Hope (Espérance)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中的一种原始激情，当我们将欲求的对象判断为可能获得时产生；与焦虑(anxiety)对立，极端形态为信心(confidence)或确信(assurance)。核心论述见 Articles 58、166。

memes.label: Imagination (Imagination)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的想象——介于感觉与知性之间的心灵官能，在激情的产生、身体影响和情感体验中发挥关键作用。

memes.label: Indignation (Indignation)
memes.description: A species of aversion/hatred felt toward those who do evil to others (not to oneself). Distinct from anger—which concerns harm directed at oneself—indignation is a moral passion central to Descartes's ethics of generosity and the regulation of anger.

memes.label: Jealousy (Jalousie)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中的一种激情，基于欲望和对所有物的高度敬重而产生的焦虑；可因对象的价值而有荣誉性或可责性之分。核心论述见 Articles 167–169。

memes.label: Joy (La Joie)
memes.description: Joy (la joie) is one of the six primitive passions in Descartes' taxonomy — the soul's enjoyment of a present good, caused by the animal spirits, with both a bodily (passional) and an intellectual form, the latter being the soul's own proper pleasure.

memes.label: Love (L'Amour)
memes.description: "Love (l'amour) is one of the six primitive passions in Descartes' taxonomy — a commotion of the soul caused by the animal spirits that inclines the soul to join itself in will to objects that appear beneficial, with two primary kinds: benevolent and concupiscent."

memes.label: "Part I: The Passions in General"
memes.description: "Articles 1–50: The Passions in General and incidentally the whole nature of man"

memes.label: "Part II: The Number and Order of the Passions"
memes.description: "Articles 51–148: The Number and Order of the Passions, and the explanation of the six primitive passions"

memes.label: "Part III: Particular Passions"
memes.description: "Articles 149–212: Particular Passions"

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: In Descartes' philosophy, a passion is a perception, sensation, or commotion of the soul caused, maintained, and strengthened by the movement of the animal spirits — distinct from both volitions (actions) and external sensations. The passions are the foundation of all the pleasures and pains of embodied life.

memes.label: Pineal Gland (Glande Pinéale)
memes.description: The pineal gland (la glande pinéale) is, in Descartes' physiology, the "principal seat of the soul" — the唯一 bodily organ where the soul directly interacts with the animal spirits, serving as the causal junction between mind and body.

memes.label: Pity (Pitié)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中混杂爱的悲伤，因看到他人遭受不应得的不幸而产生；慷慨之人与普通人的 pity 有质的区别。核心论述见 Articles 185–189。

memes.label: Regret (Regret)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的悔憾——一种针对过去善好的悲伤，与自责、自怜和羞耻密切相关。

memes.label: Remorse (Remords)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的悔恨/良心的责备——当行动犹豫不决时产生的内心不安，是悔改的前奏而非悔改本身。

memes.label: René Descartes (1596–1650)
memes.description: French philosopher, mathematician, and scientist; author of The Passions of the Soul (1649), his final published work. His mechanistic account of the passions broke with the Aristotelian-scholastic tradition.

memes.label: Repentance (Repentir)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中一种由对过去行为的自我判断引发的复合激情。Article 63 将其定义为"最痛苦的激情之一"，源于意识到自己做了坏事。不同于单纯的 remorse（懊悔），repentance 包含改正的意志。核心论述见 Articles 63、177、191。

memes.label: Sadness (La Tristesse)
memes.description: Sadness (la tristesse) is one of the six primitive passions — a languid commotion of the soul caused by the animal spirits that represents a present evil as belonging to the soul, directly opposed to joy and frequently compounded with hatred.

memes.label: Scorn (Dédain)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的蔑视——当单纯的轻视变为主动的贬低倾向时的一种激情，与崇敬对举。

memes.label: Self-Love (Amour-Propre)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的自爱——一种指向自身的爱的变体，构成多种复合激情（愤怒、嫉妒、虚荣等）的底层成分。

memes.label: Shame (Honte)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中一种由自我评价产生的复合激情。Article 205 将其定义为一种悲伤(sadness)，源于意识到自己被他人责备或谴责。与 vainglory（虚荣）构成对立，是自我意识和他人评价在情感层面的交汇。核心论述见 Articles 117、205–207。

memes.label: Soul (Âme)
memes.description: In Descartes' philosophy of mind, the soul (âme) is the thinking substance wholly distinct from the body yet intimately united to it. It is the seat of all thought — both volitions (actions) and passions — and exercises its functions principally through the pineal gland.

memes.label: The Passions of the Soul (Les Passions de l'âme, 1649)
memes.description: René Descartes' final published work (1649), a systematic treatise on the nature, classification, and governance of the passions. Written for Princess Elisabeth of Bohemia, it synthesizes mechanistic physiology with moral philosophy.

memes.label: Vainglory (Vaine Gloire)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》中讨论的虚荣——一种基于自爱的喜悦，源于对自己受到赞扬的想象或期望，与羞耻具有对称的运作结构。

memes.label: Vanity (La Vanité)
memes.description: 笛卡尔在《论灵魂的激情》第三部分中详细分析的道德心理概念。虚荣是慷慨(generosity)的对立面——一种基于对自身价值的错误高估而产生的自满。核心论述见 Articles 157-158、160、190、202-203。

memes.label: Veneration (Vénération)
memes.description: 笛卡尔激情体系中的一种复合激情，由 esteem（尊重）在对象被视为自由原因时转化而成。在 Article 162 中被集中论述，与 scorn（轻蔑）构成对立。崇敬涉及对他者自由意志的道德评价，在笛卡尔的激情伦理学中占据独特位置。

memes.label: William Harvey (1578–1657)
memes.description: English physician whose discovery of blood circulation (1628) is praised by Descartes in Article 7 as a model of empirical investigation against the blind authority of the ancients, foundational to the mechanistic physiology of The Passions of the Soul.

memes.label: Wonder (L'Admiration)
memes.description: Wonder (l'admiration) is the first of the six primitive passions in Descartes' taxonomy — a sudden surprise at something novel that has no contraries and serves the unique epistemic function of prompting the soul to learn and remember what is worth knowing.
